_template_locked_2026_05_22_by_john.historical_figures_1st_person: I am [NAME]. Today's lesson: [LESSON TITLE]. I am known for [1-line bio of legacy]. [Then the story / teaching content — keep existing first-person content]
_template_locked_2026_05_22_by_john.hall_masters: I am Master [NAME]. Welcome to [HALL NAME]. Here I [what is taught in this Hall]. Listen well.

washington_delaware.voice_name: Washington (custom) - slower, dignified pacing per John 2026-05-23
washington_delaware.opener: Imagine, for a moment, that you are a general. Your men have not been paid in months. Half of them have no shoes. The river ahead is choked with ice. And you are about to risk everything you have on one crossing, in the dark. Why? Why would any sane commander do this? Listen — and I will tell you what I chose, and why the cause of Liberty depended on it.

custer_custer.bio: leading the United States 7th Cavalry, my golden hair, my bravado on the frontier, and my last stand at the Little Bighorn in June of 1876

suntzu_suntzu.bio: writing thirteen short chapters called The Art of War, twenty-five centuries ago in ancient China, that are still read by generals, athletes, and merchants today

hannibal_cannae.bio: marching war elephants across the Alps in winter to bring Carthage to the gates of Rome, and for breaking the Roman legions at Cannae

eisenhower_dday.bio: commanding the Allied forces on the sixth of June, nineteen forty-four, the largest sea invasion in history, on the beaches of Normandy, and later serving as the thirty-fourth President of the United States

marcus_aurelius_stoicism.bio: ruling Rome as emperor for nineteen years, leading the legions on the Danube frontier, and writing my private notebook the Meditations, never meant for any eyes but my own

socrates_socrates.bio: walking the streets of Athens twenty-four hundred years ago asking simple questions that unraveled men who thought they were wise, and for drinking the hemlock rather than betraying the examined life

aristotle_aristotle.bio: studying under Plato, tutoring a boy who would become Alexander the Great, and writing on logic, biology, ethics, and politics — works the West would read for two thousand years

machiavelli_machiavelli.bio: serving the Republic of Florence as a diplomat, watching popes and princes scheme up close, and writing a short, hard little book called The Prince that told rulers how the world actually works

alexander_alexander.bio: conquering Persia, Egypt, and lands as far as the Indus by the age of thirty, never losing a battle, and founding cities that still bear my name

caesar_caesar.bio: conquering Gaul, crossing the Rubicon with the Thirteenth Legion in defiance of the Senate, and ending the Roman Republic on the steps of the Senate house

augustus_augustus.bio: becoming the first emperor of Rome at the age of nineteen, ending a century of civil wars, and founding the Pax Romana — two centuries of Roman peace

lincoln_lincoln.bio: serving as the sixteenth President of the United States, holding the Union together through four years of civil war, and ending slavery in America

churchill_churchill.bio: standing as British Prime Minister in the dark year of nineteen-forty when Britain stood alone against Hitler, and refusing — in speeches the whole free world heard — to ever surrender
